title: Creamy Earthy Venezuelan Pork Belly Stir-fry
description: This meal-prep dish features tender pork belly slices in a creamy, earthy sauce that complements the natural flavors of the vegetables.
instructions: Marinate the pork belly in a mixture of low-sodium soy sauce, coconut milk, cumin seeds, bay leaves, and water for at least 30 minutes.
Prepare vegetables: dice onions into thin slices, mince garlic finely, and slice cherry tomatoes and bell peppers thinly.
Heat olive oil in a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated pork belly to sear on both sides until golden brown, about 5-7 minutes per side.
Remove the pork from the pan and set aside. In the same pan, add sliced onions, minced garlic, and diced cherry tomatoes to sauté for about 3 minutes.
Add bell peppers and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes until slightly softened but still crisp-tender.
Return the seared pork belly to the skillet with vegetables. Add coconut milk and let it simmer for about 5 minutes while stirring occasionally, allowing flavors to meld together.
Stir in cumin seeds and bay leaves if using, and cook for another minute or so until mixture thickens slightly. Garnish with fresh cilantro before serving.
Serve hot as a main course or side dish over quinoa, rice, or vegetables.
